focus: Should .opencode/plugins/README.md remain only an entrypoint inventory, or should it be expanded because the root README points to it as the plugin contract?
noveltyJustification: Reclassified the plugin README gap into a contract-ownership decision: root README delegates to .opencode/plugins/README.md, spec-kit bridge README delegates the goal contract to goal_plugin.md, and the plugin README remains inventory/config for other plugins while mk-goal source now exposes cleanup env vars, store_health and mutation fields.

graphEvents.label: Should .opencode/plugins/README.md stay inventory-only or expand because root README calls it the plugin contract?
README.md lines 1231-1233 point goal plugin contract readers to .opencode/plugins/README.md
.opencode/plugins/README.md lines 42-50 list mk-goal.js as one entrypoint row
.opencode/plugins/README.md lines 68-149 cover config for advisor/code-graph but not mk-goal
mk-goal.js defines cleanup env vars, archive/sweep, usage_limited, store_health and mutation output
system-spec-kit plugin_bridges README says the goal plugin contract lives in references/hooks/goal_plugin.md
.opencode/plugins/README.md is too thin to be the goal plugin contract target promised by root README
ENV_REFERENCE.md still omits three mk-goal cleanup/archive env vars

label: .opencode/plugins/README.md is too thin to be the goal plugin contract target promised by root README
README surfaces split the goal plugin contract target between .opencode/plugins/README.md and goal_plugin.md
ENV_REFERENCE.md still omits MK_GOAL_STATE_ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS, MK_GOAL_STATE_ACTIVE_RETENTION_DAYS and MK_GOAL_STATE_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS
Live command filename remains .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md; .opencode/commands/goal.md is absent

direction: README surfaces claiming usage_limited is dead/unimplemented or that goal-state never gets cleaned up
reason: README-only sweeps found no current usage_limited dead/unimplemented claim and no goal-state never-cleaned claim; visible dead matches were unrelated generic prose.
